info.name: B. Getting Started with SP-API for Vendors 🤝
info.description:
# Getting Started with SP-API for Vendors

## Description

Your launchpad into the Selling Partner API as a vendor. This collection puts 13 SP-APIs at your fingertips with pre-built, ready-to-send requests — so you can go from zero to your first successful API call in minutes.

## Overview

The [Selling Partner API (SP-API)](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/what-is-the-selling-partner-api) is Amazon's REST-based API suite that lets vendors programmatically manage their retail operations on Amazon — from purchase orders and shipments to catalog management, direct fulfillment, and analytics.

📘 Learn more: [SP-API Vendor Use Cases](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/sp-api-vendor-use-cases)

## SP-APIs Included

| # | API | Documentation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2 | Catalog Items API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/catalog-items-api-v2022-04-01-reference) |
| 3 | Listings (Product Type Definitions, Listings Items, Listings Restrictions) | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/listings-items-api-v2021-08-01-reference) |
| 4 | Feeds API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/feeds-api-v2021-06-30-reference) |
| 5 | Reports API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/reports-api-v2021-06-30-reference) |
| 6 | Data Kiosk API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/data-kiosk-api-v2023-11-15-reference) |
| 7 | Notifications API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/notifications-api-v1-reference) |
| 8 | Vendor Retail Procurement API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/vendor-orders-api-v1-reference) |
| 9 | Vendor Direct Fulfillment API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/vendor-direct-fulfillment-orders-api-v2021-12-28-reference) |
| 10 | A+ Content Management API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/a-content-api-v2020-11-01-reference) |
| 11 | Uploads API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/uploads-api-v2020-11-01-reference) |
| 12 | Tokens API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/tokens-api-v2021-03-01-reference) |
| 13 | Application Management API | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/application-management-api-v2023-11-30-reference) |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- A registered SP-API application ([How to register](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/registering-your-application))
    
- Valid SP-API credentials (LWA client ID and secret)
    
- A vendor account authorized to your application
    

### Setup Steps

1. **Fork the collection** — Click "Fork" on the collection to create your own copy.
    
2. **Fork the environment** — Fork the associated [environment template](https://www.postman.com/amazon-selling-partner-api/sp-api/environment/) as well.
    
3. **Set environment variables** — Fill in your `client_id`, `client_secret`, and `refresh_token` in the environment. Make sure the `marketplace` and `base_url` match your target region.
    
4. **Assign the environment** — Select the forked environment from the environment dropdown in Postman.
    
5. **Authenticate** — The collection includes a pre-request script that automatically retrieves and stores your access token. You can also manually call **1.1 : LWA Access Token** or **1.2 : LWA Grantless Access Token** (for [grantless operations](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/grantless-operations)).
    
6. **Start exploring** — Navigate to any API folder and send requests. Use environment variables `{{variable}}` as needed for your API call parameters.
    

## Additional Resources

- [SP-API Documentation](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/)
    
- [SP-API Vendor Use Cases](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/sp-api-vendor-use-cases)
    
- [SP-API Vendor FAQ](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/vendor-faq)
    
- [SP-API Endpoints (regional)](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/sp-api-endpoints)
    
- [Marketplace IDs](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/marketplace-ids)

item.name: 1. Fork Collection & Auth
1.1 : LWA Access Token

request.description: This is to retrieve access token

item.description:
Call this endpoint to **Login with Amazon (LWA)** and retrieve an access token.  
A post-script is available and will store the returned access token to the environment variable **access_token** to then authenticate and authorize API requests.

item.name: 1.2 : LWA Grantless Access Token

urlencoded.description: Scope for the grantless access token. e.g. sellingpartnerapi::notifications

request.description: This is to retrieve access token

item.description:
Call this endpoint to **Login with Amazon (LWA)** and retrieve a _grantless_ access token.

A _grantless_ access token is required for Grantless Operations.  
Read more on Grantless operations [here](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/grantless-operations).  

A post-script is available and will store the returned access token to the environment variable **access_token** to then authenticate and authorize API requests.

1. To fork this collection, Click on **"Run in Postman"** Button or right-click the collection and click on **Create a fork.**
    
2. Fork the [environment](https://www.postman.com/amazon-selling-partner-api/sp-api/environment/) you need
    
3. Fill the environment variables. Make sure valid "client_id", "client_secret" and "refresh_token" are set.
    
4. Ensure environment variables {{variable}} are used as needed for your API calls parameters.
    

Note: the collection comes with a pre-script: a script that's excuted before each run. The script uses the authentication endpoint to retrieve the access token and store it in the access_token. The access_token value is passed in the header **x-amz-access-token.**  
In, **1.1 : LWA Access Token**, it is also possible to do this step manually. For grantless operations, make sure to manually set the access token for the relevant request. The grantless token is retrieved using the **1.1 : Lwa Grantless Access Token**

The Selling Partner API for Product Type Definitions provides programmatic access to attribute and data requirements for product types in the Amazon catalog.

Use this API to return the JSON Schema for a product type that you can then use with other Selling Partner APIs, such as the Selling Partner API for Listings Items or the Selling Partner API for Feeds (for JSON-based listing feeds) for the validation of the requirements of your submissions.

To get the next page of results, call the getFeeds operation and include this token as the only parameter. Specifying nextToken with any other parameters will cause the request to fail.

item.description:
The Selling Partner API for Feeds lets you upload data to Amazon on behalf of a selling partner.

The Feeds API is an asynchronous API and need to be used as follows:

1 - A feed document is created using the Create Feed Document operation. Data is uploaded to the document url returned.

2 - A feed is created using the Create Feed operation

3 - Get Feed is used to retrieve the feed status and details (or optionally use the FEED_PROCESSING_NOTIFICATION)

4 - Retrieve the feed processing document

---

Refer to the Feed Type Values for a list of feeds that can be used:  
[https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/feed-type-values](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/feed-type-values)

1 - Create a report

item.description:
You can schedule requests for reports so that they are submitted periodically, using the `createReportSchedule` operation. Use the period enumeration to specify the time period. To identify which reports can be scheduled, review the [Report Type Values](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/report-type-values) in the Selling Partner API documentation.

Scheduling a report is done as follows:

1 - Create a report schedule for a report type

2 - Check the reports using the **getReports** operation though. Ideally, the REPORT_PROCESSING_FINISHED notification is used to inform about newly generated instances of the scheduled report instead of polling.

3 - Use the **reportDocumentId** when available to download the report

The Selling Partner API for Reports lets you retrieve and manage a variety of reports that can help selling partners manage their businesses.

You can either request or schedule reports depending on their availability. Please note that some reports are either requested or scheduled.

---

For more information, please refer to the report type values page:

[https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/report-type-values](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/report-type-values)

item.name: 6. Data Kiosk

request.description:
Creates a Data Kiosk query request.

**Note:** The retention of a query varies based on the fields requested. Each field within a schema is annotated with a `@resultRetention` directive that defines how long a query containing that field will be retained. When a query contains multiple fields with different retentions, the shortest (minimum) retention is applied. The retention of a query's resulting documents always matches the retention of the query.

item.description: With the A+ Content API, you can build applications that help selling partners add rich marketing content to their Amazon product detail pages. A+ content helps selling partners share their brand and product story, which helps buyers make informed purchasing decisions. Selling partners assemble content by choosing from content modules and adding images and text.

For example, if you are creating an upload destination for the createLegalDisclosure operation of the Messaging API, the `{resource}` would be `/messaging/v1/orders/{amazonOrderId}/messages/legalDisclosure`, and the entire path would be `/uploads/2020-11-01/uploadDestinations/messaging/v1/orders/{amazonOrderId}/messages/legalDisclosure`. item.name: 12. Tokens API

request.description:
Returns a Restricted Data Token (RDT) for one or more restricted resources that you specify. A restricted resource is the HTTP method and path from a restricted operation that returns Personally Identifiable Information (PII), plus a dataElements value that indicates the type of PII requested. See the Tokens API Use Case Guide for a list of restricted operations. Use the RDT returned here as the access token in subsequent calls to the corresponding restricted operations.

item.description:
The Selling Partner API for Tokens provides a secure way to access a customer's PII (Personally Identifiable Information).

You can call the Tokens API to get a Restricted Data Token (RDT) for one or more restricted resources that you specify. The RDT authorizes subsequent calls to restricted operations that correspond to the restricted resources that you specified.
